1:
"And after these things I saw four angels standing on the four corners of the earth, holding the four winds of the earth, that the wind should not blow on the earth, nor on the sea, nor on any tree." The witnesses note the four angels restraining the four winds. Victorinus notes (in his reading) the four angels and the angel with the seal point to the restraining of judgment until the servants of God are sealed and established against the great persecution. The witnesses note John seeing four angels at the four corners of the earth holding the four winds, that no wind should blow on earth, sea, or tree; the restraining of judgment until God's servants are sealed — that, between the sixth and seventh seals, John sees a pause and a sealing: "four angels standing on the four corners of the earth, holding the four winds of the earth, that the wind should not blow on the earth, nor on the sea, nor on any tree"; the witnesses note the figure — the "four winds," held back by the angels, are the destroying judgments of God (war, calamity, desolation) ready to be loosed upon the world; the four angels "holding" them signify that these judgments are under God's restraint, kept back for a time so that no harm comes to "earth," "sea," or "tree" until a needful work is done; so that God, in his providence, holds back the storm of judgment until his own people are made safe; that the wind should not blow on the earth, nor on the sea, nor on any tree. The witnesses note four angels standing on the four corners of the earth, holding the four winds of the earth. The witnesses note that the wind should not blow on the earth, nor on the sea, nor on any tree.

The witnesses note the four angels restraining the four winds — the destroying judgments of God held back under his restraint, so that no harm comes until his people are sealed and made safe.

So the witnesses make the verse the restraining of judgment until the sealing. The lesson the expositors draw is that the destroying winds of judgment are held back under God's restraint until his servants are sealed. The witnesses note the four angels: "four angels standing on the four corners of the earth" — at the corners of the earth; the four angels; the the corners; the the earth; the standing; the the winds. The expositors observe the holding the winds: "holding the four winds of the earth" — restraining the winds; the holding the winds; the restraining; the the four winds; the held back; the the judgments. The witnesses press the the winds are judgments: the winds are the destroying judgments of God; the the winds are judgments; the destroying; the war; the desolation; the loosed. The expositors draw the lesson on the under restraint: under God's restraint, kept back for a time; the under restraint; the God's; the kept back; the for a time; the held. The witnesses observe the no harm: "that the wind should not blow on the earth, nor on the sea, nor on any tree" — no harm comes; the no harm; the the earth; the the sea; the the tree; the spared. The expositors note the holds the storm: God holds back the storm until his people are safe; the holds the storm; the held back; the his people; the safe; the the sealing. The witnesses draw the application: the believer marks the four angels standing on the four corners of the earth holding the four winds of the earth, the that the wind should not blow on the earth nor on the sea nor on any tree, and the restraining of judgment. The verse thus records the restraining of judgment until the sealing — teaching that the destroying winds are held back under God's restraint until his servants are sealed.
2:
"And I saw another angel ascending from the east, having the seal of the living God: and he cried with a loud voice to the four angels, to whom it was given to hurt the earth and the sea," The witnesses note the angel from the east with the seal of the living God. Victorinus notes (in his reading) the angel ascending from the east with the seal is the herald of restoration, sealing and establishing the churches before the loosing of judgment. The witnesses note John seeing another angel ascend from the east with the seal of the living God, crying to the four angels who were to hurt the earth and sea; the angel with God's seal staying the judgment to mark out God's own — that John sees a further messenger: "another angel ascending from the east, having the seal of the living God"; the witnesses note the two marks of this angel and his errand — he comes "from the east" (the quarter of light and of God's blessing) bearing "the seal of the living God" (the instrument by which God's people are to be marked as his own) — and "he cried with a loud voice to the four angels, to whom it was given to hurt the earth and the sea," charging them to stay their destroying work; the witnesses note the gracious order of God's providence — before the judgments are loosed, the sealing angel goes forth to mark out and secure God's people, that they may be known and kept; so that God ever cares for his own before he sends the storm; another angel ascending from the east, having the seal of the living God. The witnesses note another angel ascending from the east, having the seal of the living God. The witnesses note he cried with a loud voice to the four angels, to whom it was given to hurt the earth and the sea.

The witnesses note the angel from the east with the seal of the living God, charging the four angels to stay their judgment — God marking out his own before the storm is loosed.

So the witnesses make the verse the sealing angel staying the judgment. The lesson the expositors draw is that, before the judgments are loosed, an angel with God's seal marks out and secures his people. The witnesses note the another angel: "another angel ascending from the east" — from the quarter of light; the another angel; the from the east; the the light; the God's blessing; the the herald. The expositors observe the seal of God: "having the seal of the living God" — the instrument to mark God's people; the seal of God; the the instrument; the mark; the his own; the the living God. The witnesses press the cried to the four: "he cried with a loud voice to the four angels" — charging them to stay; the cried to the four; the charging; the stay; the the four angels; the the judgment. The expositors draw the lesson on the to hurt: "to whom it was given to hurt the earth and the sea" — who were to hurt the earth and sea; the to hurt; the the earth; the the sea; the the judgment; the stayed. The witnesses observe the seals first: before judgment, the sealing angel goes forth; the seals first; the before judgment; the the sealing; the goes forth; the marks. The expositors note the cares for his own: God cares for his own before the storm; the cares for his own; the his own; the before the storm; the secured; the kept. The witnesses draw the application: the believer marks the another angel ascending from the east having the seal of the living God, the he cried with a loud voice to the four angels to whom it was given to hurt the earth and the sea, and the sealing angel. The verse thus records the sealing angel staying the judgment — teaching that, before the judgments are loosed, God marks out and secures his people.
3:
"Saying, Hurt not the earth, neither the sea, nor the trees, till we have sealed the servants of our God in their foreheads." The witnesses note the charge to stay the judgment until God's servants are sealed. Gill notes (in the historicist reading) the commission to hurt was not yet to be carried out, but at the appointed time, at the blowing of the trumpets, the winds were let loose; the sealing comes first. The witnesses note John hearing the angel say, "Hurt not the earth, the sea, or the trees, till we have sealed the servants of our God in their foreheads"; the staying of all harm until God's servants are marked on the forehead — that the sealing angel's word is recorded: "Hurt not the earth, neither the sea, nor the trees, till we have sealed the servants of our God in their foreheads"; the witnesses note the purpose and the manner of the sealing — the judgments are held back until "the servants of our God" are "sealed," marked out as his own and secured against the coming harm, and that "in their foreheads" (an open, visible mark of ownership, as the Spirit seals believers and sets God's mark upon them); the witnesses note the comfort — God will not let the storm fall until every one of his own is sealed and safe; so that the believer is divinely marked and kept, distinguished and preserved amid the judgments that fall on the world; till we have sealed the servants of our God in their foreheads. The witnesses note Hurt not the earth, neither the sea, nor the trees. The witnesses note till we have sealed the servants of our God in their foreheads.

The witnesses note the charge to stay all harm until God's servants are sealed in their foreheads — marked as his own and secured; the storm not falling until every one of his own is safe.

So the witnesses make the verse the sealing of God's servants before judgment. The lesson the expositors draw is that the judgments are held back until God's servants are marked and secured as his own. The witnesses note the hurt not: "Hurt not the earth, neither the sea, nor the trees" — Gill notes the harm held back; the hurt not; the held back; the the earth; the the sea; the the trees. The expositors observe the till sealed: "till we have sealed the servants of our God" — until God's servants are sealed; the till sealed; the the servants; the of God; the marked; the secured. The witnesses press the in their foreheads: "in their foreheads" — an open, visible mark of ownership; the in their foreheads; the open; the visible; the mark; the ownership. The expositors draw the lesson on the the Spirit seals: as the Spirit seals believers and sets God's mark; the the Spirit seals; the believers; the God's mark; the sets; the marked. The witnesses observe the held back until safe: the storm held back until his own are safe; the held back until safe; the the storm; the his own; the safe; the sealed. The expositors note the marked and kept: the believer is divinely marked and kept; the marked and kept; the divinely; the distinguished; the preserved; the amid judgment. The witnesses draw the application: the believer marks the Hurt not the earth neither the sea nor the trees, the till we have sealed the servants of our God in their foreheads, and the sealing of God's servants. The verse thus records the sealing of God's servants before judgment — teaching that the judgments are held back until God's servants are marked and secured.
4:
"And I heard the number of them which were sealed: and there were sealed an hundred and forty and four thousand of all the tribes of the children of Israel." The witnesses note the number of the sealed — 144,000 of the tribes of Israel. Gill notes John heard the number, and so could be sure of the exact count, which did not depend on his sight and telling, in which some mistake might be made. The witnesses note John hearing that the number sealed was a hundred and forty-four thousand of all the tribes of the children of Israel; the definite, complete number of the sealed drawn from Israel's tribes — that John, having seen the sealing, now "heard the number of them which were sealed: and there were sealed an hundred and forty and four thousand of all the tribes of the children of Israel"; the witnesses note the significance of the number — 144,000 (twelve thousand from each of twelve tribes) is a definite, ordered, and complete number, marking that God knows and secures the exact tale of his own, not one missing; the witnesses note the debate on "the tribes of the children of Israel" — some take it of literal, ethnic Israel, a remnant of the Jews sealed and preserved; others of the spiritual Israel, the whole church of God under the figure of the twelve tribes, the symbolic number standing for the full and perfect number of the elect; either way, the sealed are a complete and known company, kept by God; there were sealed an hundred and forty and four thousand. The witnesses note I heard the number of them which were sealed. The witnesses note an hundred and forty and four thousand of all the tribes of the children of Israel.

The witnesses note the number sealed — 144,000 of the tribes of Israel — a definite, complete number, with debate whether it means literal ethnic Israel or the whole spiritual Israel, the church.

So the witnesses make the verse the complete number of the sealed. The lesson the expositors draw is that God knows and secures the exact, complete number of his own, none missing. The witnesses note the heard the number: "I heard the number of them which were sealed" — Gill notes he heard it, so was sure of the count; the heard the number; the heard; the sure; the the count; the exact. The expositors observe the 144,000: "an hundred and forty and four thousand" — twelve thousand from each tribe; the 144,000; the twelve thousand; the each tribe; the definite; the complete. The witnesses press the ordered and complete: a definite, ordered, complete number; the ordered and complete; the definite; the ordered; the complete; the the tale. The witnesses present the debate: the witnesses note the debate on "the tribes of Israel" — some take it of literal ethnic Israel, a remnant of the Jews; others of the spiritual Israel, the whole church under the figure of the tribes. The expositors draw the lesson on the not one missing: God knows the exact tale, not one missing; the not one missing; the the exact tale; the his own; the secured; the known. The witnesses observe the complete company: the sealed are a complete, known company; the complete company; the known; the kept; the by God; the secured. The witnesses draw the application: the believer marks the I heard the number of them which were sealed, the an hundred and forty and four thousand of all the tribes of the children of Israel, and the complete number. The verse thus records the complete number of the sealed — teaching that God knows and secures the exact, complete number of his own.
5:
"Of the tribe of Juda were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Reuben were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Gad were sealed twelve thousand." The witnesses note the sealing of twelve thousand from each of the first tribes named, beginning with Judah. Gill notes Judah is mentioned first because Christ sprang from that tribe, and the pure worship of God was preserved in it; its name signifies "praise." The witnesses note John recording twelve thousand sealed of Judah, of Reuben, and of Gad; the equal sealing of each tribe, Judah first for Christ's sake — that John begins the roll of the sealed tribe by tribe: "of the tribe of Juda were sealed twelve thousand," and so of Reuben and Gad; the witnesses note two things — first, the equal number, "twelve thousand" from each tribe, marking that none is favoured or neglected, each having its full and equal share among the sealed; and second, the order, that "Juda" is named first, not by natural birthright (for Reuben was the firstborn), but because of Christ, who "sprang out of Juda," and because the worship of God was preserved in it; the witnesses note that Judah's name signifies "praise," a fitting head for the company of the sealed who are gathered to praise God; so that the sealing is orderly, equal, and headed by the tribe of the Messiah; of the tribe of Juda were sealed twelve thousand. The witnesses note of the tribe of Juda were sealed twelve thousand. The witnesses note of the tribe of Reuben... of the tribe of Gad were sealed twelve thousand.

The witnesses note twelve thousand sealed of each tribe — equal and full — Judah named first not by birthright but for Christ who sprang from it; the sealing orderly and headed by the Messiah's tribe.

So the witnesses make the verse the equal sealing of the tribes, Judah first. The lesson the expositors draw is that each tribe has its full, equal share among the sealed, Judah named first for Christ's sake. The witnesses note the twelve thousand each: "of the tribe of Juda were sealed twelve thousand" — equal number from each tribe; the twelve thousand each; the equal; the each tribe; the full share; the none neglected. The expositors observe the none favoured: none favoured or neglected; the none favoured; the none neglected; the equal share; the each; the full. The witnesses press the Judah first: Judah named first, not by birthright; the Judah first; the not birthright; the Reuben firstborn; the named first; the Christ. The expositors draw the lesson on the Christ sprang from it: because Christ sprang out of Judah; the Christ sprang from it; the Christ; the out of Judah; the the Messiah; the the tribe. The witnesses observe the worship preserved: and the worship of God preserved in it; the worship preserved; the God's worship; the in Judah; the pure; the kept. The expositors note the praise: Judah's name signifies "praise," fitting for the sealed; the praise; the the name; the signifies; the fitting; the the sealed. The witnesses draw the application: the believer marks the of the tribe of Juda were sealed twelve thousand, the of the tribe of Reuben and of Gad were sealed twelve thousand, and the equal sealing. The verse thus records the equal sealing of the tribes, Judah first — teaching that each tribe has its full, equal share, Judah named first for Christ's sake.
6:
"Of the tribe of Aser were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Nepthalim were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Manasses were sealed twelve thousand." The witnesses note the continuing roll of the sealed tribes — Asher, Naphtali, and Manasses. Gill notes Asher, which the Septuagint pronounce "Aser," signifies "blessed," as all the sealed ones are, blessed with all spiritual blessings, with grace here and glory hereafter. The witnesses note John recording twelve thousand sealed of Asher, of Naphtali, and of Manasses; the roll of the sealed continued, each tribe's name bearing a fitting sense — that John continues the equal sealing through the next tribes: "of the tribe of Aser were sealed twelve thousand," and so of Naphtali and Manasses; the witnesses note that the commentators (Gill especially) observe a fitness in the very names — "Asher" signifies "blessed," fitting for the sealed who are blessed with all spiritual blessings, grace now and glory to come; the witnesses note the steady, equal continuance of the count, twelve thousand from each, the full number gathered from every tribe; so that the sealing proceeds without favour or omission, each tribe yielding its appointed company of the redeemed, the names themselves whispering the blessedness of those whom God has sealed; of the tribe of Aser were sealed twelve thousand. The witnesses note of the tribe of Aser were sealed twelve thousand. The witnesses note of the tribe of Nepthalim... of the tribe of Manasses were sealed twelve thousand.

The witnesses note the roll continued — Asher, Naphtali, Manasses — each twelve thousand; the names themselves fitting, "Asher" meaning "blessed," whispering the blessedness of the sealed.

So the witnesses make the verse the continued equal sealing of the tribes. The lesson the expositors draw is that the sealing proceeds without favour, each tribe yielding its full company, the names fitting the blessedness of the sealed. The witnesses note the Asher sealed: "of the tribe of Aser were sealed twelve thousand" — Gill notes Asher signifies "blessed"; the Asher sealed; the Asher; the blessed; the the name; the fitting. The expositors observe the blessed: the sealed are blessed with all spiritual blessings; the blessed; the spiritual blessings; the grace; the glory; the the sealed. The witnesses press the Naphtali and Manasses: "of the tribe of Nepthalim... of Manasses" — the roll continued; the Naphtali and Manasses; the continued; the the roll; the each tribe; the twelve thousand. The expositors draw the lesson on the equal count: the equal count, twelve thousand each; the equal count; the twelve thousand; the each; the full; the steady. The witnesses observe the no omission: without favour or omission; the no omission; the no favour; the each tribe; the appointed; the full. The expositors note the names whisper: the names themselves whisper the blessedness of the sealed; the names whisper; the the names; the blessedness; the the sealed; the fitting. The witnesses draw the application: the believer marks the of the tribe of Aser were sealed twelve thousand, the of the tribe of Nepthalim and of Manasses were sealed twelve thousand, and the continued sealing. The verse thus records the continued equal sealing of the tribes — teaching that the sealing proceeds without favour, each tribe yielding its full company.
7:
"Of the tribe of Simeon were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Levi were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Issachar were sealed twelve thousand." The witnesses note the further roll — Simeon, Levi, and Issachar — each twelve thousand sealed. Gill notes Simeon signifies "hearing," and such a hearing as is attended with obedience, denoting the spiritual and practical hearing of the gospel by those who hear, understand, believe, and practise. The witnesses note John recording twelve thousand sealed of Simeon, of Levi, and of Issachar; the roll continued, the names again whispering the graces of the sealed — that John continues: "of the tribe of Simeon were sealed twelve thousand," and so of Levi and Issachar; the witnesses note again the fitness the commentators find in the names — "Simeon" signifies "hearing," the spiritual, obedient hearing of the gospel that marks the truly sealed, who hear, understand, believe, and obey; the witnesses note that even Levi, the priestly tribe, has its twelve thousand, none excluded from the sealed company; the steady equal count goes on, each tribe yielding its full number; so that the sealing of God's people is complete, orderly, and gathered from every quarter, the very names of the tribes adorned with the graces of those whom God seals — hearing, joining, and the rest; of the tribe of Simeon were sealed twelve thousand. The witnesses note of the tribe of Simeon were sealed twelve thousand. The witnesses note of the tribe of Levi... of the tribe of Issachar were sealed twelve thousand.

The witnesses note the roll continued — Simeon, Levi, Issachar — each twelve thousand; the names again fitting, "Simeon" meaning "hearing," the obedient hearing of the gospel that marks the sealed.

So the witnesses make the verse the continued sealing through Simeon, Levi, Issachar. The lesson the expositors draw is that each tribe yields its full number, the names fitting the graces of the sealed. The witnesses note the Simeon sealed: "of the tribe of Simeon were sealed twelve thousand" — Gill notes Simeon signifies "hearing"; the Simeon sealed; the Simeon; the hearing; the the name; the fitting. The expositors observe the obedient hearing: the spiritual, obedient hearing of the gospel; the obedient hearing; the spiritual; the the gospel; the believe; the obey. The witnesses press the Levi included: "of the tribe of Levi" — even the priestly tribe has its number; the Levi included; the the priestly; the its number; the none excluded; the sealed. The expositors draw the lesson on the Issachar: "of the tribe of Issachar" — the roll continued; the Issachar; the continued; the the roll; the twelve thousand; the each. The witnesses observe the steady count: the steady equal count goes on; the steady count; the equal; the each tribe; the full; the goes on. The expositors note the complete and orderly: the sealing is complete, orderly, from every quarter; the complete and orderly; the complete; the orderly; the every quarter; the gathered. The witnesses draw the application: the believer marks the of the tribe of Simeon were sealed twelve thousand, the of the tribe of Levi and of Issachar were sealed twelve thousand, and the continued sealing. The verse thus records the continued sealing through Simeon, Levi, Issachar — teaching that each tribe yields its full number, the names fitting the graces of the sealed.
8:
"Of the tribe of Zabulon were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Joseph were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Benjamin were sealed twelve thousand." The witnesses note the closing of the roll — Zebulun, Joseph, and Benjamin — completing the 144,000. Gill notes Zebulun signifies "dwelling," and was the tribe in which Christ had his dwelling and much conversed, denoting his gracious inhabitation among the saints and sealed. The witnesses note John recording twelve thousand sealed of Zebulun, of Joseph, and of Benjamin, completing the number; the roll of the sealed brought to its full and complete sum — that John closes the list: "of the tribe of Zabulon were sealed twelve thousand," and so of Joseph and Benjamin, completing the twelve tribes and the full 144,000; the witnesses note once more the fitness in the names — "Zebulun" signifies "dwelling," the tribe where Christ much dwelt, fitting for the sealed in whom Christ and the Father and the Spirit graciously dwell; the witnesses note the completion of the sealing — every tribe has now yielded its twelve thousand, the whole company of the sealed gathered and sure, not one tribe omitted, not one of the appointed number lacking; so that the sealing of God's people is full, complete, and final, every one of his own marked and kept before the judgments fall; of the tribe of Benjamin were sealed twelve thousand. The witnesses note of the tribe of Zabulon were sealed twelve thousand. The witnesses note of the tribe of Joseph... of the tribe of Benjamin were sealed twelve thousand.

The witnesses note the roll closed — Zebulun, Joseph, Benjamin — completing the 144,000; the sealing full and final, every tribe yielding its number, every one of God's own marked and kept.

So the witnesses make the verse the completion of the sealing. The lesson the expositors draw is that the sealing is brought to its full sum, every tribe yielding its number, the whole company sealed and sure. The witnesses note the Zebulun sealed: "of the tribe of Zabulon were sealed twelve thousand" — Gill notes Zebulun signifies "dwelling"; the Zebulun sealed; the Zebulun; the dwelling; the the name; the fitting. The expositors observe the Christ dwelt: the tribe where Christ much dwelt; the Christ dwelt; the the tribe; the much dwelt; the gracious inhabitation; the the sealed. The witnesses press the Joseph and Benjamin: "of the tribe of Joseph... of Benjamin" — the roll closed; the Joseph and Benjamin; the closed; the the roll; the completing; the twelve thousand. The expositors draw the lesson on the full sum: completing the twelve tribes and the full 144,000; the full sum; the the twelve tribes; the the 144,000; the complete; the full. The witnesses observe the not one lacking: not one tribe omitted, not one number lacking; the not one lacking; the no tribe omitted; the the appointed; the complete; the full. The expositors note the full and final: the sealing is full, complete, and final; the full and final; the complete; the final; the every one; the marked. The witnesses draw the application: the believer marks the of the tribe of Zabulon were sealed twelve thousand, the of the tribe of Joseph and of Benjamin were sealed twelve thousand, and the completed sealing. The verse thus records the completion of the sealing — teaching that the sealing is brought to its full sum, the whole company sealed and sure.
